summary:
The Ilekta step involves referring this case directly towards In Vitro Fertilisation due to persistent bilateral tubal obstruction despite long-standing fertility issues; surgical interventions have shown lower efficacy compared to assisted reproductive technologies like IVF based on recent medical research findings. To summarize:

The recommended next step after confirming bilateral tubal blockages via HSG is immediate referral to IVF as it offers better chances of pregnancy than attempted surgery considering her prolonged infertility period without resolution through conventional methods. Recent data suggest that such cases benefit most from bypassing traditional treatments aimed at restoring fallopian tube function by opting straightaway for advanced reproduction techniques including IVF procedures. 

Note how both summaries are concise while capturing essential information regarding treatment options post-diagnosis of specific female factor infertility conditions involving blocked tubes. Both versions emphasize prioritizing modern assisted reproductive technology over potentially futile attempts at repairing damaged anatomy when faced with longstanding unexplained sterility accompanied by radiographic confirmation of obstructive pathology within the upper genital tract system responsible for egg transport necessary prior fertilization can occur naturally inside uterus lining environment during ovulation cycles under normal circumstances absent any structural impediments along its path leading up toward site where sperm meets released oocyte(s).
